api/v3/request.purpose: Pure HTTP request parsing and query-parameter conversion utilities for the v3 API layer. Translates raw HTTP inputs (JSON bodies, sort strings, API filter types) into typed internal domain types before they reach handlers. Contains no business logic.

patterns.name: Return *apierrors.BaseAPIError not error
patterns.description: All parse functions return *apierrors.BaseAPIError (nilable pointer), not the Go error interface. Callers check for nil to detect parse failure and propagate structured API errors directly.

patterns.name: Contains operator wraps to SQL LIKE pattern
patterns.description: api.QueryFilterString.Contains/Ncontains are converted to LIKE/NLIKE via filter.ContainsPattern which escapes SQL metacharacters (%, _, \). Never pass Contains values directly to filter.FilterString.Like.
patterns.example: Like: convertContainsOperator(source.Contains) // wraps to %value% with escaping

patterns.name: Recursive filter conversion for And/Or
patterns.description: And/Or slices in QueryFilterString are converted recursively via convertQueryFilterStringList so nested boolean expressions are fully translated.

patterns.name: SortBy text format: 'field [asc|desc]'
patterns.description: ParseSortBy parses a string with UnmarshalText: one or two whitespace-separated tokens. First token is Field, second (optional) is Order; default order is asc. Validate() is called inside UnmarshalText — callers must not call it separately.

patterns.name: Nil-safe pointer converters
patterns.description: ConvertQueryFilterStringPtr returns nil when given nil, avoiding nil-dereference in optional filter fields.

patterns.name: API Neq maps to internal Ne
patterns.description: The API field name Neq maps to internal filter field Ne (names differ). QueryFilterStringMapItem additionally has an Exists field not present in QueryFilterString.
patterns.example: Ne: source.Neq // API uses Neq, internal type uses Ne

key_file_guides.role: JSON body decoder. ParseOptionalBody silently ignores io.EOF (empty body) to allow PATCH semantics.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ParseOptionalBody leaves payload unchanged on empty body — callers must treat unchanged payload as 'no update', not 'zero value'.

key_file_guides.role: Converts api.QueryFilterString and QueryFilterStringMapItem to pkg/filter.FilterString. Handles Contains->Like wrapping and recursive And/Or.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Neq maps to Ne (field name differs between API and internal type). Contains maps to Like with % wrapping; do not set Like directly from Contains.

key_file_guides.role: SortBy value type with UnmarshalText; ToSortxOrder converts to pkg/sortx.Order.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Validate() is called inside UnmarshalText — callers must not skip or duplicate validation. ErrSortFieldRequired fires when Field is empty after parsing.

anti_patterns: Returning the Go error interface instead of *apierrors.BaseAPIError from a parse function
Setting filter.FilterString.Like directly from an API Contains value without calling filter.ContainsPattern
Calling Validate() separately after ParseSortBy — it is already called inside UnmarshalText
Adding business logic or domain service calls in this package — it is pure translation/parsing only
Passing Contains string directly to internal filter without % wrapping — ContainsPattern must be applied

decisions.decision: Parse functions return *apierrors.BaseAPIError instead of error
decisions.rationale: Allows handlers to directly propagate structured API errors with field path and source metadata without wrapping or type-asserting.

decisions.decision: Contains wraps to SQL LIKE pattern inside this package
decisions.rationale: Centralises metacharacter escaping so handlers never need to know about SQL LIKE syntax; all filter construction uses filter.ContainsPattern consistently.

code_examples.scenario: Handler converts API filter query param to internal FilterString

api/v3/response.purpose: Reusable response envelope types for the v3 API: cursor-based and page-based pagination wrappers. All list endpoints must use one of these two structs to ensure consistent JSON shape across the API.

patterns.name: Generic pagination wrappers via constructors
patterns.description: Both CursorPaginationResponse[T] and PagePaginationResponse[T] are generic over item type T. Handlers call the constructor (NewCursorPaginationResponse / NewPagePaginationResponse) instead of building the struct manually.

patterns.name: Cursor derived from Item interface
patterns.description: NewCursorPaginationResponse requires T to implement pagination.Item (exposes Cursor() method). First/Last cursors are set from items[0] and items[len-1]; Next is only set when a full page was returned.
patterns.example: func NewCursorPaginationResponse[T pagination.Item](items []T, pageSize int) CursorPaginationResponse[T]

patterns.name: nullable.Nullable for next/previous links
patterns.description: CursorMetaPage.Next and Previous are nullable.Nullable[string] (not *string) so they serialize as explicit JSON null when absent rather than being omitted entirely.
patterns.example: Next: nullable.NewNullNullable[string]() // produces JSON null, not omitted

patterns.name: Next cursor gated on full page
patterns.description: Next cursor is only populated when len(items) == pageSize; a partial page signals last page. Do not set Next manually from caller code.

key_file_guides.role: Cursor-based pagination envelope; T must implement pagination.Item from pkg/pagination/v2.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Next cursor is only populated when len(items) == pageSize; a partial page signals last page. Do not set Next manually.

key_file_guides.role: Offset/page-number pagination envelope; PageMetaPage.Total is optional (*int) for cases where total count is expensive.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Caller must populate PageMetaPage fully (Size, Number, Total) before passing to NewPagePaginationResponse — no defaults are inferred.

anti_patterns: Returning a raw []T from a list endpoint instead of wrapping in CursorPaginationResponse or PagePaginationResponse
Setting CursorMetaPage.Next to a non-null value when fewer items than pageSize were returned
Using *string for Next/Previous fields — must use nullable.Nullable[string] to produce explicit JSON null
Building CursorPaginationResponse struct manually instead of calling NewCursorPaginationResponse

decisions.decision: Cursor encoded directly from the last item in the page rather than stored separately
decisions.rationale: Avoids out-of-sync cursor state; the item itself is the canonical source of its own cursor position.

decisions.decision: nullable.Nullable[string] used for Next/Previous instead of *string
decisions.rationale: Produces explicit JSON null when absent rather than omitting the field, which is required by AIP pagination contracts for consistent client-side handling.

code_examples.scenario: List handler wraps domain items in cursor pagination response

api/v3/server.purpose: The sole assembly point for the v3 API surface: validates Config, constructs all domain handler structs, registers OAS request validation middleware on a Chi router, and delegates every generated ServerInterface method to the appropriate typed handler. Credits feature flag is enforced both at constructor time (noop wiring) and at route dispatch time.

patterns.name: Config.Validate() before NewServer
patterns.description: Config.Validate() is called at the top of NewServer and returns a wrapped error listing all missing required services. Never skip validation.
patterns.example: if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid v3 server config: %w", err) }

patterns.name: credits.enabled gated at route level AND constructor level
patterns.description: Credits routes in routes.go check both s.Credits.Enabled and handler != nil before dispatching. In NewServer, when credits are disabled, noop implementations replace real services. Both guards are required.
patterns.example: if !s.Credits.Enabled || s.customersCreditsHandler == nil { unimplemented.GetCustomerCreditBalance(w, r, ...); return }

patterns.name: Handler delegation via .With(params).ServeHTTP(w, r)
patterns.description: Each ServerInterface method calls the typed handler method, optionally passes path/query params via .With(...), and calls ServeHTTP. No business logic lives in routes.go.

patterns.name: resolveNamespace closure injected into every handler
patterns.description: Namespace is not a URL path segment; a resolveNamespace func(ctx) (string, error) closure is constructed once in NewServer and injected into all handler constructors.
patterns.example: resolveNamespace := func(ctx context.Context) (string, error) { ns, ok := config.NamespaceDecoder.GetNamespace(ctx); ... }

patterns.name: Optional handlers guarded by nil checks
patterns.description: Handlers for optional services (ChargeService, LLMCostService, CostService) are only constructed when the service is non-nil; the server field stays nil otherwise. Routes must nil-check or delegate to api.Unimplemented{}.
patterns.example:
var chargesH chargeshandler.Handler
if config.ChargeService != nil { chargesH = chargeshandler.New(...) }

patterns.name: Content negotiation at route level
patterns.description: QueryMeter inspects Accept header via commonhttp.GetMediaType; text/csv routes to the CSV handler, all other types use JSON handler. Content negotiation is done in routes.go, not in the handler.

patterns.name: var _ api.ServerInterface compile-time assertion
patterns.description: A blank-identifier compile-time assertion ensures Server implements the generated ServerInterface. Must be kept; removing it hides missing route implementations.

key_file_guides.role: Config struct definition, Config.Validate, Server struct with all handler fields, NewServer constructor, RegisterRoutes with OAS validation middleware.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new domain service requires: field in Config, nil-check in Validate (if required), handler construction in NewServer, handler field in Server struct. Missing any step causes a panic or silent noop.

key_file_guides.role: One method per generated ServerInterface operation; pure delegation to handler. Contains all credits feature-flag guards.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Credits endpoints must check both s.Credits.Enabled and handler != nil. Multi-param routes pack args into a local struct before .With(...). Never add business logic here.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic or error handling inside routes.go — it must be pure delegation
Forgetting credits.Enabled guard on new credit-related routes (both route and constructor levels required)
Passing nil handler to ServeHTTP — always guard optional handlers with nil checks or delegate to api.Unimplemented{}
Adding a service to Config without adding it to Config.Validate() — silently accepts broken wiring
Removing the var _ api.ServerInterface compile-time assertion

decisions.decision: credits.enabled enforced at both NewServer (noop wiring) and route dispatch (explicit guard)
decisions.rationale: Credits is cross-cutting; a single guard point is insufficient. The route-level check is a safety net for cases where noop wiring is bypassed.

decisions.decision: resolveNamespace is a closure injected into handlers, not middleware
decisions.rationale: Namespace decoding is not a URL path parameter in v3; injecting it as a closure into each handler keeps the transport layer clean without requiring Chi context injection.

decisions.decision: Handler fields are unexported; Config services are exported
decisions.rationale: Handlers are implementation details assembled in NewServer; the Config struct is the public dependency surface that callers (app/common) populate via Wire.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new domain handler to the v3 server

api/v3/templates.purpose: A patch file that customises the oapi-codegen Chi middleware template to route deepObject 'filter' query parameters through a custom filters.Parse parser instead of the default runtime.BindQueryParameterWithOptions. Ensures union filter types are correctly parsed during code generation.

patterns.name: Patch targets ParamName=filter + Style=deepObject exclusively
patterns.description: The template patch adds a conditional branch: only parameters named 'filter' with style 'deepObject' use the custom path. All other parameters continue using the standard oapi-codegen runtime binder.

key_file_guides.role: Applied during make gen-api to override oapi-codegen's Chi middleware template for filter parameter handling.
key_file_guides.watch_for: This patch must be re-applied after any oapi-codegen version upgrade. If filter params regress to standard binding, union filter types will silently break. The custom parser is filters.Parse — ensure that import is present in the generated middleware.

anti_patterns: Editing the generated chi-middleware output directly instead of maintaining this patch
Widening the patch condition beyond ParamName=filter — standard deepObject params must use runtime.BindQueryParameterWithOptions

decisions.decision: Custom parser injected via template patch rather than post-generation sed
decisions.rationale: Template patches survive oapi-codegen regeneration as long as the template structure is stable; post-generation edits are wiped on every make gen-api run.

api/v3/test.purpose: Integration test fixtures and tests for the v3 API filter parsing and OAS validation pipeline. Provides an embedded test OpenAPI spec (openapi.test.yaml) and end-to-end tests that spin up a real Chi+oasmiddleware server to verify every filter type is correctly validated and parsed by filters.Parse.

patterns.name: Embedded test spec via //go:embed
patterns.description: openapi.test.yaml is embedded as []byte via embed.go and loaded by tests through openapi3.NewLoader().LoadFromData(apiv3test.OpenAPITestSpec). Tests never read the file from disk at runtime.

patterns.name: newTestServer helper bootstraps full validation stack
patterns.description: Test helpers construct a real Chi router with oasmiddleware.ValidateRequest middleware and httptest.NewServer. Tests exercise the full request path including OAS schema validation before reaching the handler.
patterns.example: srv := newTestServer(t, noopHandler) // spins up Chi+oasmiddleware with embedded spec

patterns.name: Two-phase test structure: validation then parse
patterns.description: TestFieldFilterValidation asserts OAS schema acceptance/rejection (204/400). TestFieldFilterParse additionally runs filters.Parse and asserts the resulting typed Go struct. Both must pass for a valid filter.
patterns.example: // noopHandler for validation tests; parseHandler for parse tests

patterns.name: t.Context() for all test contexts
patterns.description: All context values in tests use t.Context() (tied to test lifecycle), never context.Background().

key_file_guides.role: Embeds openapi.test.yaml as OpenAPITestSpec []byte for use in tests.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Package name is 'test' (not 'test_test'); tests in filters_test.go use package 'test_test' to test as external consumer.

key_file_guides.role: Minimal OpenAPI 3.0 spec defining the FieldFilters schema with all supported filter types (BooleanFieldFilter, NumericFieldFilter, StringFieldFilter, etc.) mapped to x-go-type from api/v3/filters package.
key_file_guides.watch_for: x-go-type-import and x-go-type annotations on each schema drive which Go type oapi-codegen generates. Adding a new filter type here requires a matching type in api/v3/filters.

key_file_guides.role: End-to-end filter validation and parse tests covering all filter types and operators in both short form (filter[field]=value) and object form (filter[field][op]=value).
key_file_guides.watch_for: ULID and DateTime pattern/format violations surface as anyOf-collapse errors (rule: 'anyOf'), not per-branch errors, due to kin-openapi behaviour. Labels bare scalar (non-map) is rejected at validator level.

anti_patterns: Reading openapi.test.yaml from the filesystem at test time — always use the embedded OpenAPITestSpec
Using context.Background() in tests — use t.Context() throughout
Adding production API endpoints to openapi.test.yaml — it is a minimal fixture for filter type testing only

decisions.decision: Separate test spec (openapi.test.yaml) rather than reusing the production spec
decisions.rationale: A minimal spec isolates filter type validation tests from the full API surface, making tests faster to load and focused on filter behaviour only.
